LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CLEAVER-BROOKS', A DIVISION OF AQUA-CHEM, INC., MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF'S TESTIMONY ESTABLISHED THAT HE WORKED WITH DEFENDANT'S PRODUCT AND DEFENDANT'S EFFORT TO DISTINGUISH BETWEEN ORIGINAL AND REPLACEMENT PARTS FAILS FOR TWO REASONS. DEFENDANT DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1350 AND DEFENDANT'S QUESTIONS FAIL TO ASK A SPECIFIC QUESTION ADDRESSING DECEDENT'S EXPOSURE TO ORIGINAL GASKETS AS OPPOSED TO REPLACEMENT GASKETS. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BW/IP, INC.'S, AND ITS WHOLLY-OWNED SUBSIDIARIES, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DECEDENT'S TESTIMONY ESTABLISHES EXPOSURE TO DEFENDANT'S PRODUCT WITH ASBESTOS. DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES DO NOT DISTINGUISH BETWEEN ORIGINAL EQUIPMENT AND REPLACEMENT EQUIPMENT. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM RELATED TO INTENTIONAL TORTS IS BASED ON A FAILURE TO WARN AND DEFENDANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE THAT CLAIM. AGAIN, DEFENDANT ARGUES THAT IT HAD NO DUTY TO WARN OF DANGERS INHERENT IN A PRODUCT MANUFACTURED OR SUPPLIED BY OTHERS BUT DEFENDANT'S DISCOVERY DOES NOT CLARIFY THAT IS WHAT PLAINTIFF'S ARE RELYING UPON. THE SOPHISTICATED USER DEFENSE WAS NOT RAISED IN DEFENDANT'S MOVING PAPERS. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION IS OVERRULED. THE COURT HAS NOT RULED ON THE BURGER DECLARATION BECAUSE DEFENDANT DID NOT SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. COUNSEL GIVEN COPIES OF THE COURT'S RULINGS ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LESLIE CONTROLS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO PROVE ITS CASE OF EXPOSURE TO A LESLIE PRODUCT CONTAINING ASBESTOS PLACED ON THE PRODUCT BY LESLIE. DECEDENT'S TESTIMONY RELATED TO THE RANGER CREATES A REASONABLE INFERENCE THAT THE LESLIE PRODUCT WAS ORIGINAL EQUIPMENT AND THE RECORD REFLECTS EVIDENCE THAT LESLIE VALVES CONTAINED ASBESTOS. DEFENDANT'S INTERROGATORY 17 BY THE USE OF THE PHRASE "ORIGINAL OR REPLACEMENT" FAILS TO REQUIRE PLAINTIFF DELINEATE WHAT EVIDENCE PLAINTIFF HAS TO PROVE EITHER THAT DECEDENT WAS EXPOSED TO ORIGINAL EQUIPMENT OR REPLACEMENT EQUIPMENT. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUES 1 AND 2 FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. IT IS GRANTED AS TO ISSUES 3, 4 AND 5. PLAINTIFFS' GENERAL RESPONSES FAIL TO SET FORTH FACTS ESTABLISHING A BASIS FOR THESE CLAIMS AND PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SUBMITTED EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600 *** THIS RULING VACATED PER ORDER FILED ON JUL-9-2009***

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. RULING:COUNSEL REQUEST THAT THE MATTER BE TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS THE MATTER HAS RESOLVED, COURT DENIES SAID REQUEST AS IT WAS NOT MADE IN A TIMELY MANNER. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFFS ARE UNABLE TO PROVE THAT DECEDENT WAS EXPOSED TO DEFENDANT'S PRODUCT THAT CONTAINED AN ASBESTOS COMPONENT. DECEDENT WORKED WITH AND TOOK APART COMPRESSORS MANUFACTURED BY DEFENDANT AND DEFENDANT HAS ADMITTED THAT CERTAIN OF ITS COMPRESSORS CONTAINED ASBESTOS COMPONENTS. DEFENDANT'S INTERROGATORY 17 USES THE PHRASE "ORIGINAL OR REPLACEMENT" AND DOES NOT CLARIFY THE RECORD AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF IS CONTENDING THE EQUIPMENT WORKED ON WAS ORIGINAL OR REPLACEMENT. THE RECORD ALSO IS SILENT ON THE AGE OF ANY OF THE EQUIPMENT AT ISSUE. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUES 1 AND 2 FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. IT IS GRANTED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S DEVOID INTERROGATORY RESPONSES ON THIS ISSUE. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS PETER GALASSI, LOUISE GALASSI, DEANNE HILBURN AND DEBRA GALASSI'S MOTION UNDER CCP SECTION 473 TO VACATE ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT CRANE CO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. COUNSEL INVOLVED IN THIS CASE ARE EXPERIENCED ASBESTOS LITIGATORS. ON BOTH SIDES OF THE CASE, COUNSEL REGULARLY ARGUE FOR STRICT COMPLIANCE WITH THE MANDATES OF CCP SECTION 437C AND MOTIONS OFTEN TURN ON LITERAL COMPLIANCE WITH THE PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS OF CCP SECTION 437C. THIS CASE IS AN EXAMPLE. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S FAILURE TO SEEK A TIMELY CONTINUANCE IS UNFORTUNATE FOR THE CLIENTS. BUT IF THIS EXCUSE WERE ACCEPTED, IT WOULD UNDERMINE THE CONSTRAINTS OF CCP SECTION 437C. THE COURT ALSO FINDS THE HOLDING IN ENGLISH V. IKON BUSINESS SOLUTIONS, INC. (2001) 94 CAL. APP. 4TH 130, 138 PERSUASIVE. THE BASIS FOR THE COURT'S RULING GRANTING SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS THE FAILURE OF COUNSEL TO SEEK A CONTINUANCE PRIOR TO THE DUE DATE FOR THE OPPOSITION. AS A CONSEQUENCE, THE OPPOSITION WAS NOT TIMELY FILED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Defendant Buffalo Pumps, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Courts Order Of April 9, 2009 Denying Buffalo Pumps Motion For Summary Judgment

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Denial Of Its Motion For Summary Judgment Heard On April 17, 2008; Pursuant To Availability Of The Honorable Patrick J. Mahoney
Off Calendar Jun-15-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Tentative Ruling:
The motion is denied. The motion for reconsideration fails to address, let alone alter, the reason the motion initially was denied. Leslie Control's special interrogatories, in particular number 17, fail to shift the burden of producing evidence on the issue of new or replacement parts. As a consequence, defendant has not demonstrated the plaintiff is unable to prove that decedent was exposed to an original Leslie Control's product.

Dept 403 Judge Patrick J. Mahoney
Clerk: Theresa Santos Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600

Christopher Strunk present for Leslie Controls Inc. Paul Cook present for the plaintiffs. After argument and submission, Court concludes that Leslie Controls presented undisputed facts that shifted the burden of producing evidence to plantiffs based on the special interrogatory responses and the deposition testimony of Mr. Galassi and Captain Burger. In response, plaintiff failed to present evidence that created a triable issue of fact. Mr. Strunk will prepare order.

Notice Of Motion And Motion Under Ccp Sectio 473 To Vacate Order Granting Defendant Crane Co.S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-14-2008 Advanced From Oct-31-08 To Oct-28-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion Under Ccp Sectio 473 To Vacate Order Granting Defendant Crane Co.S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-28-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Paul Cook of Waters Kraus (310.414.8146) for plaintiffs and moving parties
Michele Barnes of K & L Gates (249.1011) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFFS PETER GALASSI, LOUISE GALASSI, DEANNE HILBURN AND DEBRA GALASSI'S MOTION UNDER CCP SECTION 473 TO VACATE ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT CRANE CO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Counsel involved in this case are experienced asbestos litigators. On both sides of the case, counsel regularly argue for strict compliance with the mandates of CCP section 437c and motions often turn on literal compliance with the procedural requirements of CCP section 437c. This case is an example. Plaintiff's counsel's failure to seek a timely continuance is unfortunate for the clients. But if this excuse were accepted, it would undermine the constraints of CCP section 437c. The Court also finds the holding in English v. IKON Business Solutions, Inc. (2001) 94 Cal. App. 4th 130, 138 persuasive. The basis for the Court's ruling granting summary judgment is the failure of counsel to seek a continuance prior to the due date for the opposition. As a consequence, the opposition was not timely filed. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Off Calendar Apr-18-2008 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-17-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: MICHAEL B. GURIEN, ESQUIRE (WATERSKRAUS, 310.414.8146) APPEARAING FOR PLAINTIFF. TIM KILLELEA, ESQUIRE (GORDON & REES, 415.986.5900) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: COUNSEL REQUEST THAT THE MATTER BE TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS THE MATTER HAS RESOLVED, COURT DENIES SAID REQUEST AS IT WAS NOT MADE IN A TIMELY MANNER. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: denied for failure to shift the burden of proof. Defendant has failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs are unable to prove that decedent was exposed to defendant's product that contained an asbestos component. Decedent worked with and took apart compressors manufactured by defendant and defendant has admitted that certain of its compressors contained asbestos components. Defendant's interrogatory 17 uses the phrase "original or replacement" and does not clarify the record as to whether plaintiff is contending the equipment worked on was original or replacement. The record also is silent on the age of any of the equipment at issue.
The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 for failure to shift the burden of proof. It is granted as to punitive damages based on plaintiff's devoid interrogatory responses on this issue. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-17-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LESLIE CONTROLS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: MICHAEL B. GURIEN, ESQUIRE (WATERSKRAUS, 310.414.8146) APPEARAING FOR PLAINTIFF. TIM KILLELEA ESQUIRE (GORDON & REES, 415.986.5900) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: denied for failure to shift the burden of proof. Defendant has failed to demonstrate that plaintiff is unable to prove its case of exposure to a Leslie product containing asbestos placed on the product by Leslie. Decedent's testimony related to the Ranger creates a reasonable inference that the Leslie product was original equipment and the record reflects evidence that Leslie valves contained asbestos. Defendant's interrogatory 17 by the use of the phrase "original or replacement" fails to require plaintiff delineate what evidence plaintiff has to prove either that decedent was exposed to original equipment or replacement equipment.
The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 for failure to shift the burden of proof. It is granted as to issues 3, 4 and 5. Plaintiffs' general responses fail to set forth facts establishing a basis for these claims and plaintiffs have not submitted evidence creating a triable issue of fact. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-10-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BW/IP, INC.'S, AND ITS WHOLLY-OWNED SUBSIDIARIES, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: PAUL C. COOK, ESQUIRE (WATERSKRAUS, 310.414.8146) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. TODD M. THACKER, ESQUIRE (JACKSON & WALLACE, 415.982.6300) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: is denied for failure to shift the burden of proof. Decedent's testimony establishes exposure to defendant's product with asbestos. Defendant's special interrogatories do not distinguish between original equipment and replacement equipment. The motion for summary adjudication is denied for failure to shift the burden of proof. Plaintiff's claim related to intentional torts is based on a failure to warn and defendant has not demonstrated that plaintiff cannot prove that claim. Again, defendant argues that it had no duty to warn of dangers inherent in a product manufactured or supplied by others but defendant's discovery does not clarify that is what plaintiff's are relying upon.
The sophisticated user defense was not raised in defendant's moving papers.
Defendant's objection is overruled. The Court has not ruled on the Burger Declaration because defendant did not shift the burden of proof. COUNSEL GIVEN COPIES OF THE COURT'S RULINGS ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-10-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WARREN PUMPS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: MICHAEL B. GURIEN, ESQUIRE (WATERSKRAUS, 310.414.8146) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. LAURIE HEPLER, ESQUIRE (CARROLL BURDICK & MCDONOUGH, 415.989.5900) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
LATE TENTATIVE RULING: The motion for summary judgment is denied. Decedent described working with defendant's product. Defendant's discovery does not distinguish between original and replacement equipment and so defendant has not shifted the burden of proof on this issue. Defendant has failed to establish that the pumps at issue were manufactured as the specific direction of the government or that the government limited or precluded warnings. Based on the decision in In Re: Hawaii Federal Asbestos cases (1992) 960 F.2d 806, the defense is not applicable to defendant's product. Defendant's objection 1 is overruled. The Court has not ruled on the other objections that relate to the Burger Declaration because defendant did not shift the burden of proof.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT TAKES THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-09-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BUFFALO PUMPS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: MICHAEL B. GURIEN, ESQUIRE (WATERSKRAUS, 310.414.8146) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. TODD M. THACKER, ESQUIRE (JACKSON & WALLACE, 415.982.6300) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
LATE TENTATIVE RULING: The motion is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of proof. Defendant's special interrogatories 6b and 6c request plaintiffs to set forth facts supporting the contention that defendant "supplied those components as original or replacement parts." This question does not require plaintiffs to delineate whether plaintiffs intend to prove decedent's exposure was to original parts, replacement parts or both. In substance, this question adds nothing to clarify plaintiffs' response to a "state all facts" interrogatory. In response to defendant's interrogatory 6c regarding the age of the machinery, plaintiffs refers to naval records and so that evidence is available for defendant to rely upon to the extent defendant wishes to present evidence on this point. Plaintiffs' discovery establishes that decedent worked with and around defendant's products and it is plaintiff's contention that the products decedent worked with and around contained asbestos. Defendant has failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs cannot prove this claim. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the claims for false representation and intentional tort because defendant did not direct discovery to those specific claims. The motion is granted as to punitive damages. Defendant's objections A and B are overruled. The Court has not ruled on the objection C because it has not relied on the Burgher declaration.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVIE RULING: BUFFALO PUMPS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. The motion for summary adjudication is denied IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART (SEE TENTATIVE RULING ABOVE). (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of proof.
